title: Steampunk Bathhouse Mechanical Plumbing System
description: The user designs a steampunk bathhouse where a gear-driven plumbing system controls water flow to an ornate bathing chamber. Players must configure interconnected gear trains to regulate water temperature, pressure, and drainage by placing, rotating, and coupling gears along pipe routes, while managing a conservation law where total mechanical energy across all gear trains remains constant—boosting one subsystem reduces another.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 with GLTFLoader and OrbitControls from CDN. Load 'shared_assets/gears.glb' and 'shared_assets/bath_day.glb'. Scene: A cross-section view of a Victorian steampunk bathhouse interior. Place the bath_day model centered on a raised marble platform. Arrange three pipe routes (hot water, cold water, drainage) leading to the bath, each controlled by a gear train slot system. Camera: perspective at 45-degree angle, OrbitControls with damping enabled, constrained to prevent going below floor. Lighting: warm ambient light (0xffe4c4), two point lights simulating gas lamps, and a directional light for shadows. Interactions (10-14 steps): 1) Click gear slots along pipes to place cloned gear models (max 8 gears from a sidebar inventory). 2) Scroll wheel on placed gears to resize them (3 sizes: small/medium/large), which affects flow rate. 3) Click-drag to rotate gear orientation snapping to 45-degree increments. 4) Adjacent gears auto-mesh and counter-rotate visually when coupled. 5) Conservation law: total gear-teeth engagement points across all three pipe routes must equal exactly 24—adding teeth to one route forces reduction elsewhere, shown via a HUD energy bar split into three colored segments. 6) Right-click a gear to remove it (undo placement). 7) Press Z for full undo, Y for redo, maintaining a history stack of all placement/resize/rotate actions. 8) When a valid configuration is set, pressing a master lever (clickable UI element) animates all gears spinning and water particles (simple sphere sprites) flowing through pipes to fill the bath. Display a top-right HUD showing temperature (hot/cold gear ratio), pressure (total gear count), and drain rate. Bath water level rises using a translucent blue plane with animated vertex displacement. Success condition: fill bath to marked line with temperature between 35-40C shown on a thermometer gauge. Add subtle steam particle effects above the bath when warm water flows.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  gearsPlaced: number,         // total number of gears currently placed in all pipe routes (0-8)
  hotWaterGearCount: number,         // number of gears placed on hot water pipe route
  coldWaterGearCount: number,         // number of gears placed on cold water pipe route
  drainageGearCount: number,         // number of gears placed on drainage pipe route
  totalTeethEngaged: number,         // sum of gear teeth engagement points across all routes (must equal 24)
  hotWaterFlowRate: number,         // calculated flow rate for hot water pipe (0.0-1.0)
  coldWaterFlowRate: number,         // calculated flow rate for cold water pipe (0.0-1.0)
  drainageFlowRate: number,         // calculated flow rate for drainage pipe (0.0-1.0)
  waterTemperature: number,         // calculated bath water temperature in Celsius (15-45)
  waterPressure: number,         // calculated system pressure in arbitrary units (0-100)
  gearsRotating: boolean,         // whether any gears are currently animating rotation
  systemBalanced: boolean,         // whether all three routes have proper gear coupling and flow
}
